title: Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
body:
Dear,
I try to use podman (from centos 7 container) instead of docker within our internal gitlab server.
But podman fail during the CI with this message:

```
$ groupadd podman
$ usermod -aG podman root
$ echo "${CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}" | podman login -u "${CI_REGISTRY_USER}" --password-stdin "${CI_REGISTRY}"
Login Succeeded!
$ container=$(./builder | tail -1)
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
$ buildah commit ${container} ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-snapshot
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
```
On the build machine I change the value of `user.max_user_namespaces` 0 to 15 000

```
$ sysctl user.max_user_namespaces
user.max_user_namespaces = 15000
``` 

But the error persist

My gitlab-ci.yml

```
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - release

variables:
  STORAGE_DRIVER:       vfs
  BUILDAH_FORMAT:       docker
  CENTOS_VERSION:       7
  VENDOR:               someone

.rpm_build:
  before_script:
    - yum install -y epel-release
    - yum install -y centos-packager


build:centos:devel:
  stage: build
  image: centos:${CENTOS_VERSION}
  tags: [docker]
  before_script:
    - yum install -y buildah podman libvarlink-util
    - groupadd podman
    - usermod -aG podman root
    - echo "${CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}" | podman login -u "${CI_REGISTRY_USER}" --password-stdin "${CI_REGISTRY}"
  script:
    - container=$(./builder-devel | tail -1)
    - buildah commit ${container} ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-devel-snapshot
    - podman push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-devel-snapshot


build:centos:
  stage: build
  image: centos:${CENTOS_VERSION}
  tags: [docker]
  before_script:
    - yum install -y buildah podman libvarlink-util
    - groupadd podman
    - usermod -aG podman root
    - echo "${CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}" | podman login -u "${CI_REGISTRY_USER}" --password-stdin "${CI_REGISTRY}"
  script:
    - container=$(./builder | tail -1)
    - buildah commit ${container} ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-snapshot
    - podman tag ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-snapshot ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest
    - podman push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-snapshot
    - podman push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest


test:centos:devel-container:
  stage: test
  image: ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-devel-snapshot
  script:
    - scl --help


release:centos:
  stage: release
  image: centos:${CENTOS_VERSION}
  tags: [docker]
  before_script:
    - yum install -y buildah podman libvarlink-util
    - echo "${CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}" | podman login -u "${CI_REGISTRY_USER}" --password-stdin "${CI_REGISTRY}"
  only:
    - tags
  script:
    - echo 'kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1' > /etc/sysctl.d/userns.conf
    - podman pull ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-devel-snapshot
    - podman pull ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-snapshot
    - podman tag  ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-devel-snapshot ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-devel
    - podman tag  ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-snapshot ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}
    - podman push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}-devel
    - podman push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CENTOS_VERSION}

```

Thanks for your help

NB: if you need I can provides the content of scripts: `builder` and `builder-devel`

comments.body: You don't have user namespaces enabled

thanks @Luap99 

How to enable it ?

is there a security issue to enable it ?
I think `sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1` should do it

This is seccomp rules in docker blocking the unshare sysctl.

--security-opt seccomp=unconfined
or
--security-opt seccomp=/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json (Containers-common package).
Does it work? Can we close this issue?

@Luap99  and @rhatdan thanks for your help and time

We try this on host machine which is centos 7 as the container

```
# sysctl -w kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone=1
sysctl: cannot stat /proc/sys/kernel/unprivileged_userns_clone: No such file or directory
```
@bioinfornatics Did my suggestion get you any further?

@rhatdan I will do, my informatics service ask if it is secure to enable those things as by default they are disabled .
and I have no idea

Running with SECCOMP disabled is less then secure.  using podman's seccomp filters, I do not believe is risky.

Setting the sysctl to allow the user of user namespace, I don't think is risky at this point, all Newer Linux have User Namespace on by default.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since the issues have been answered, I am going to close.  Reopen or open a new issue, if there are further problems.
